records.ety: [L. complicatus, p. p. of complicare to fold together. See Complex.]

defs: Composed of two or more parts united; complex; complicated; involved.
quotes: How poor, how rich, how abject, how august, How complicate, how wonderful is man!

defs: Folded together, or upon itself, with the fold running lengthwise.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Complicated; p. pr. & vb. n. Complicating.]

defs: To fold or twist together; to combine intricately; to make complex; to combine or associate so as to make intricate or difficult.
quotes: Nor can his complicated sinews fail.
Avarice and luxury very often become one complicated principle of action.
When the disease is complicated with other diseases.
